basic_details.job_title: Haryana High Court Clerk Vacancy 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1265 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Society for Centralized Recruitment of Staff in Subordinate Courts (S.S.S.C.) under High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh
basic_details.department: District and Sessions Courts across the State of Haryana
basic_details.total_vacancies: 1265 (1015 existing as on 31.12.2025 + 250 anticipated up to 30.06.2026)
basic_details.application_mode: Online only at www.sssc.gov.in under "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana"
basic_details.short_information: Haryana High Court is recruiting 1265 Clerk posts for Subordinate Courts of Haryana with Bachelor's degree qualification (BA/BSc or equivalent) and computer proficiency, last date 09 July 2026.

important_dates.qualification cut-off date: 23-06-2026 (closing date for submission of online applications)
important_dates.ews certificate validity required: Valid for FY 2026-2027, based on gross annual income for FY 2025-2026, issued on or before 23-06-2026
important_dates.demo / mock test availability: 15 days prior to date of examination (on www.sssc.gov.in)
important_dates.exam_date: To be notified on www.sssc.gov.in (exam can be held anywhere in Northern India)
important_dates.document verification / interaction date: To be notified after written exam and CPT results

application_fee.details: Fee is non-refundable and must be paid online only. The Online Application Form will be submitted only after payment of fees. Application fee for "Others" (Transgender) category is applicable as per their category in the fee table below.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The upper age limit for any candidate (except Ex-servicemen) shall not exceed 52 years even after availing all applicable age relaxations combined. Reservation benefits of age relaxation are only for bonafide residents of Haryana.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (09-07-2026). All particulars including category and sub-category once filled by the applicant in the Online Application Form shall be sacrosanct and no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process.
qualification.domicile: As per Haryana Subordinate Courts Establishment (Recruitment and General Conditions of Service) Rules, 1997. Reservation benefits only for bonafide Haryana residents with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates.
qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor of Arts (BA) or Bachelor of Science (BSc) or equivalent degree from a recognized university. AND proficiency in computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).

selection_process: Sr. No. Subject Type No. of Questions Maximum Marks Qualifying Marks

1 General Knowledge Objective (MCQ) 50 questions × 1 mark each 50 33% in each subject individually; AND minimum 40% aggregate of both subjects. English Composition (Subjective) checked only for candidates who score 33%+ in General Knowledge.
2 English Composition Objective (MCQ) 50 questions × 1 mark each 20
Subjective (typed on computer) i. Essay (150 words) 10
ii. Letter 05
iii. Precis 05
iv. Translation from Hindi to English 10
Total 100 Minimum 40% aggregate
Part Test Duration Marks Qualifying Marks
Part I Spread Sheet Test 10 minutes 10 marks 40% or more (4 or more marks)

Part II Computer Typing Test in English (Word Processing) Stipulated time Qualifying 30 WPM (Words Per Minute). Formula: (Words Typed − Mistakes) ÷ 10. Part II checked only for those who qualify Part I.

Selection is through a 3-stage process: Online Written Examination (Computer Based Test) → Computer Proficiency Test (qualifying) → Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction. The final Select List is prepared strictly on the basis of merit in the Written Examination (CBT) only. Computer Proficiency Test marks are NOT counted in the final merit. Success in examination confers no right of appointment unless the Competent Authority is satisfied after enquiry.
Stage 1 - Online Written Examination / Computer Based Test (CBT)

The CBT is conducted online (Computer Based Test format) in the following subjects. The exam may be held on different dates for different sets of candidates (multiple shifts). Normalization of objective scores using Standard Deviation Method will be applied if exam is held in multiple shifts.

Negative Marking: 1/4th mark deducted for every wrong answer in MCQ portion. Questions not attempted receive no credit or penalty. Subjective English Composition will be typed on computer within the stipulated time.
Tie-break Rule: If two or more candidates obtain same marks
the elder in age is ranked higher. If age is also same
candidates are placed alphabetically (A to Z) in the merit list.

Answer Key & Objections: Provisional Answer Key will be uploaded on www.sssc.gov.in after CBT. Candidates can raise objections at Rs. 25/- per objection (non-refundable). Cross-objections against proposed changes in provisional answer key are also allowed. Objections/cross-objections must include proposed correct answer and supporting reasons with documentary proof.
Stage 2 - Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) - Qualifying Nature Only
After CBT results
a category-wise determined number of candidates will be called for CPT. CPT marks are NOT counted in final merit - it is purely qualifying in nature.
PwBD Exemption from CPT: PwBD with Both Arms Affected (BA) can apply for full exemption from CPT with Disability Certificate. PwBD as defined under Section 2(r) of RPwD Act

2016 can apply for CPT exemption with Disability Certificate + Medical Certificate from CMO/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent of a Government healthcare institution certifying physical limitation to type at 30 WPM. PwBD under Section 2(s) of RPwD Act can apply for exemption with Medical Certificate only. All such applications must be submitted within 15 days of declaration of CBT result.
Stage 3 - Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction

Candidates who qualify both CBT and CPT (category-wise as decided by the High Court) will be called for checking of original testimonials/interaction. No TA/DA shall be paid for appearing at any stage of the recruitment process including this stage. The Select List will be prepared strictly on the basis of CBT merit.
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply in online mode only at www.sssc.gov.in under the head "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana". No other means/mode of submission of application will be accepted. The online application form is submitted only after payment of fees. All applicants (including reserved category) can submit only one application per category - if more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered.
Before starting the Online Application Form, keep the following ready:
Personal details (Name, Date of Birth, Category, Sub-category etc.)
Valid and active Email ID (keep unchanged throughout the process - do not use email of unknown persons)
Valid and active mobile number (keep unchanged throughout the process)
Online payment facility - Internet Banking / Debit Card / Credit Card / UPI
Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old, against white background)
Scanned signature
Computer/device with inbuilt or attached webcam (for real-time photograph capture during application)
Documents to be Uploaded in Online Application Form (before 09-07-2026):
Real-time photograph captured via webcam/camera at time of filling form
Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old)
Scanned signature
All other requisite documents as specified in the Online Application Form
Documents to be Produced at Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction:
Printout of Online Application Form (retain 5 copies of the photograph uploaded in the application)
E-Admit Card (downloaded and printed from www.sssc.gov.in, duly self-attested / attested by a Gazetted Officer)
Date of Birth certificate / Matriculation certificate
Bachelor's degree certificate and mark sheets
Matriculation mark sheet showing Hindi/Sanskrit as a subject (or +2/BA/MA mark sheet showing Hindi)
Computer proficiency proof
Caste certificate (in prescribed format, issued by competent authority of Haryana Government, valid as on 23-06-2026) - for SC/DSC/OSC/BC-A/BC-B/EWS candidates
BC category undertaking that no change has occurred in their status and they do not fall in Creamy Layer up to 23-06-2026
EWS Income & Assets Certificate (valid for FY 2026-2027, based on FY 2025-2026 income, issued on or before 23-06-2026, on prescribed Haryana Government proforma)
Disability Certificate (for PwBD candidates - in prescribed format as per RPwD Act, 2016)
ESM Identity Card from Zila Sainik Board / Discharge Certificate or Book
ESM Family Member Eligibility Certificate from Zila Sainik Board (valid as on 23-06-2026)
Sports Certificate (for ESP candidates - as per applicable Haryana Government orders)
Freedom Fighter Certificate from competent authority (for Freedom Fighter / children / grandchildren)
No Objection Certificate (for in-service Government employees - from Head of Office/Department)
Experience Certificate of equivalent post from Appointing Authority (for adhoc/contract/daily wages employees availing age relaxation)
Any other documents in support of eligibility/category claimed in application

For Re-checking of Answer Sheets (if required after CBT result): Written request to Member Secretary, S.S.S.C., High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh, along with fee of Rs. 500/- per answer sheet (Indian Postal Order payable to S.S.S.C.), within 30 days of declaration of final result. Re-evaluation is not allowed - only re-checking for totalling errors.
Apply Online at: www.sssc.gov.in → Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana → Registration Form → Application Form → Fee Payment

For Technical queries: Helpline No. +91 99866-38762 (Working days, 09:30 AM – 05:00 PM). For terms and conditions queries: Phone 0172-2722012 / 9115898394 (Working days, 09:30 AM – 05:00 PM). All corrigenda/notices will be notified on www.sssc.gov.in only - newspaper advertisements, if any, are secondary; the SSSC website content prevails.

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Society for Centralized Recruitment of Staff in Subordinate Courts (S.S.S.C.) under High Court of Punjab and Haryana, on behalf of the District and Sessions Judges in the State of Haryana, has officially released Detailed Employment Notice No. 36C/SSSC/HR/2026 dated 22/05/2026 for filling up 1265 posts of Clerks in the Subordinate Courts of Haryana by direct recruitment. The notification includes complete details on category-wise vacancies (including backlog vacancies), age limit, educational qualification, fee, mode of selection (Computer Based Test + Computer Proficiency Test), normalization of marks, and application procedure. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification and the Haryana Subordinate Courts Establishment (Recruitment and General Conditions of Service) Rules, 1997 before applying. Download SSSC Haryana Clerk Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF Extended SSSC Haryana Clerk Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: Haryana High Court is recruiting 1265 Clerk posts for Subordinate Courts of Haryana with Bachelor's degree qualification (BA/BSc or equivalent) and computer proficiency, last date 09 July 2026.

As per latest rules and instructions of Government of Haryana duly adopted for Subordinate Courts of Haryana
Online only at www.sssc.gov.in under "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana"
Online Written Examination / Computer Based Test (CBT) + Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) + Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction

Bachelor's degree (BA/BSc or equivalent) from a recognized university + Computer proficiency (Word Processing & Spread Sheets) + Hindi/Sanskrit in Matriculation or +2/BA/MA. Candidates must give undertaking to work anywhere in the State of Haryana.

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 1265 posts of Clerks are announced - 1015 existing vacancies as on 31.12.2025 and 250 anticipated vacancies up to 30.06.2026. These are distributed category-wise as per Haryana Government reservation norms including backlog vacancies being advertised for the 2nd and 3rd time. The number of vacancies may be increased or decreased without notice depending on vacancies at the time of preparation of the merit list. The High Court will decide the number of candidates to be recommended. PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible Important Notes on Reservation: Reservation benefits (age relaxation, fee concession, and category reservation) are available ONLY to bonafide residents of Haryana State. Candidates from reserved categories of other states must apply under General category (except age relaxation for Government employees as provided in Rules). Freedom Fighters' reservation is available only from unfilled ESM or BC quota (maximum 2% overall). Transgender applicants ("Others" in Gender column) must hold certificate of identity issued by District Magistrate under The Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019.

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (09-07-2026). All particulars including category and sub-category once filled by the applicant in the Online Application Form shall be sacrosanct and no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process.

rows.details: As per Haryana Subordinate Courts Establishment (Recruitment and General Conditions of Service) Rules, 1997. Reservation benefits only for bonafide Haryana residents with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates.
Bachelor of Arts (BA) or Bachelor of Science (BSc) or equivalent degree from a recognized university. AND proficiency in computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).
Must have passed Matriculation with Hindi or Sanskrit as one of the subjects OR must have done +2 / BA / MA with Hindi as one of the subjects, before the cut-off date of qualification (23-06-2026).

No person who has entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a spouse living, or who (having a spouse living) has entered into a marriage with any person shall be eligible. Exception: only if such marriage is permissible under the personal law applicable to such person as certified by the Hon'ble High Court.

In-service employees of Punjab/Haryana Government, other State Government, Government of India, High Court, Subordinate Courts, or Union Territory Chandigarh must obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their Head of Office or Department and produce it when called. Failure to produce NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.
All applicants must give an undertaking in the Online Application Form that they will work anywhere in the State of Haryana wherever they are posted.

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The upper age limit for any candidate (except Ex-servicemen) shall not exceed 52 years even after availing all applicable age relaxations combined. Reservation benefits of age relaxation are only for bonafide residents of Haryana.

rows.category: PwBD - Persons with Disabilities (Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016)
rows.maximum_age_relaxation: 10 years relaxation (+5 additional years if PwBD belongs to SC/ST/BC/EWS). Overall maximum: 52 years. Relaxation applicable irrespective of whether post is reserved for PwBD, provided the post is identified suitable for the relevant disability category.

Actual age minus (actual period of military service + up to 3 years of break) - if resultant age does not exceed prescribed maximum. Condition: must have rendered continuous military service for not less than 6 months before release; and released otherwise than by dismissal/discharge on account of misconduct or inefficiency.

rows.category: Unmarried Women / Wives of Disabled Military Personnel / Widowed or Legally Divorced Women / Judicially Separated Women (residing separately for more than 2 years)
Adhoc / Contract / Work-charged / Daily Wages employees of Haryana Government Departments/Boards/Corporations (including Govt.-aided institutions)
rows.maximum_age_relaxation: Relaxation equal to number of completed years of service on equivalent post (excluding break period), subject to maximum 52 years. Cannot avail the same benefit again for subsequent appointment if already appointed on regular basis with this relaxation.

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fee is non-refundable and must be paid online only. The Online Application Form will be submitted only after payment of fees. Application fee for "Others" (Transgender) category is applicable as per their category in the fee table below.

rows.category: SC (DSC) / SC (OSC) / BC-A / BC-B / ESM / EWS of Haryana (Male & Female)
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) of Haryana

content_sections.heading: Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection is through a 3-stage process: Online Written Examination (Computer Based Test) → Computer Proficiency Test (qualifying) → Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction. The final Select List is prepared strictly on the basis of merit in the Written Examination (CBT) only. Computer Proficiency Test marks are NOT counted in the final merit. Success in examination confers no right of appointment unless the Competent Authority is satisfied after enquiry. Stage 1 - Online Written Examination / Computer Based Test (CBT) The CBT is conducted online (Computer Based Test format) in the following subjects. The exam may be held on different dates for different sets of candidates (multiple shifts). Normalization of objective scores using Standard Deviation Method will be applied if exam is held in multiple shifts. Negative Marking: 1/4th mark deducted for every wrong answer in MCQ portion. Questions not attempted receive no credit or penalty. Subjective English Composition will be typed on computer within the stipulated time. Tie-break Rule: If two or more candidates obtain same marks, the elder in age is ranked higher. If age is also same, candidates are placed alphabetically (A to Z) in the merit list. Answer Key & Objections: Provisional Answer Key will be uploaded on www.sssc.gov.in after CBT. Candidates can raise objections at Rs. 25/- per objection (non-refundable). Cross-objections against proposed changes in provisional answer key are also allowed. Objections/cross-objections must include proposed correct answer and supporting reasons with documentary proof. Stage 2 - Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) - Qualifying Nature Only After CBT results, a category-wise determined number of candidates will be called for CPT. CPT marks are NOT counted in final merit - it is purely qualifying in nature. PwBD Exemption from CPT: PwBD with Both Arms Affected (BA) can apply for full exemption from CPT with Disability Certificate. PwBD as defined under Section 2(r) of RPwD Act, 2016 can apply for CPT exemption with Disability Certificate + Medical Certificate from CMO/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent of a Government healthcare institution certifying physical limitation to type at 30 WPM. PwBD under Section 2(s) of RPwD Act can apply for exemption with Medical Certificate only. All such applications must be submitted within 15 days of declaration of CBT result. Stage 3 - Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction Candidates who qualify both CBT and CPT (category-wise as decided by the High Court) will be called for checking of original testimonials/interaction. No TA/DA shall be paid for appearing at any stage of the recruitment process including this stage. The Select List will be prepared strictly on the basis of CBT merit.

content_sections.heading: Who should NOT apply for Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Candidates who do not hold a Bachelor's degree (BA/BSc or equivalent) from a recognized university - it is the mandatory minimum educational qualification. Candidates who have not passed Matriculation with Hindi or Sanskrit as one of the subjects, AND have not done +2/BA/MA with Hindi as one of the subjects - both the degree AND the Hindi/Sanskrit condition must be fulfilled by 09-07-2026. Candidates who are not bonafide residents of Haryana - reservation benefits (category, age relaxation, fee concession) are available only to Haryana residents with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates. Non-Haryana reserved category candidates must apply under General category. Candidates who do not have proficiency in computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets) - this is a mandatory qualification and is tested in the CPT. Candidates above 42 years of age as on 01.01.2026 (for General category), except those eligible for specific age relaxation as per the rules - overall age cap is 52 years for most relaxation categories. Candidates who have already married a person having a living spouse, or who (having a living spouse) have married another person - unless the Hon'ble High Court has specifically exempted such marriage under applicable personal law. In-service Government employees who cannot produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their Head of Office or Department when called - failure to produce NOC will result in cancellation of candidature. Candidates who cannot or will not commit to working anywhere in the State of Haryana - a mandatory undertaking to this effect must be given in the Online Application Form. Candidates who entered incorrect information in the application - all particulars are sacrosanct after submission and no change is permitted at any stage; errors cannot be corrected or explained later. Candidates who are unable to type the English Composition Subjective portion on computer and cannot qualify the CPT Typing Test at 30 WPM (unless eligible for CPT exemption under PwBD provisions).

content_sections.heading: Common mistakes / What to check before applying SSSC Haryana Clerk Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Not verifying Haryana domicile status before applying under a reserved category - benefits of reservation and fee concession are only for bonafide Haryana residents; applicants from other states' reserved categories must apply under General category. Not checking that both degree qualification AND Hindi/Sanskrit condition are fulfilled before applying - missing either condition will result in disqualification. Filling incorrect category or sub-category in the Online Application Form - all particulars are sacrosanct after submission and no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process. OBC/BC candidates from Creamy Layer applying under BC reservation - only Non-Creamy Layer candidates (as per Haryana Govt. Notification No. 40/13/2024-1SW dated 16.07.2024) are eligible for BC reservation; Creamy Layer candidates must apply as General. EWS candidates not having the correct certificate - EWS certificate must be valid for FY 2026-2027, based on gross annual income for FY 2025-2026, issued on or before 23-06-2026. EWS certificates issued for Central Government jobs will NOT be accepted. ESM and family member candidates not having a valid eligibility certificate from the concerned Zila Sainik Board - this must be valid as on the last date of application (09-07-2026). Uploading a photograph that is more than one month old or does not match the real-time photo captured during application or the photo to be pasted on the E-Admit Card - mismatch will result in denial of entry at the examination centre. Not uploading proper photograph and signature - applications without proper photograph and signature will be summarily rejected. Expecting the E-Admit Card to be sent by post - E-Admit Cards are uploaded on www.sssc.gov.in only; candidates must download and print them by logging in with Registration ID and Password. Not checking the SSSC website and registered email (including spam folder) regularly - all notifications, exam dates, admit card updates and results are communicated only through www.sssc.gov.in and email/SMS; SSSC is not responsible for any candidate failing to appear due to non-checking of the website. Waiting until the last date to apply - SSSC advises applying well before the closing date to avoid internet/website congestion issues; the cut-off date is sacrosanct and no relief will be granted after the deadline under any circumstance.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must apply in online mode only at www.sssc.gov.in under the head "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana". No other means/mode of submission of application will be accepted. The online application form is submitted only after payment of fees. All applicants (including reserved category) can submit only one application per category - if more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered. Before starting the Online Application Form, keep the following ready: Personal details (Name, Date of Birth, Category, Sub-category etc.) Valid and active Email ID (keep unchanged throughout the process - do not use email of unknown persons) Valid and active mobile number (keep unchanged throughout the process) Online payment facility - Internet Banking / Debit Card / Credit Card / UPI Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old, against white background) Scanned signature Computer/device with inbuilt or attached webcam (for real-time photograph capture during application) Documents to be Uploaded in Online Application Form (before 09-07-2026): Real-time photograph captured via webcam/camera at time of filling form Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old) Scanned signature All other requisite documents as specified in the Online Application Form Documents to be Produced at Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction: Printout of Online Application Form (retain 5 copies of the photograph uploaded in the application) E-Admit Card (downloaded and printed from www.sssc.gov.in, duly self-attested / attested by a Gazetted Officer) Date of Birth certificate / Matriculation certificate Bachelor's degree certificate and mark sheets Matriculation mark sheet showing Hindi/Sanskrit as a subject (or +2/BA/MA mark sheet showing Hindi) Computer proficiency proof Caste certificate (in prescribed format, issued by competent authority of Haryana Government, valid as on 23-06-2026) - for SC/DSC/OSC/BC-A/BC-B/EWS candidates BC category undertaking that no change has occurred in their status and they do not fall in Creamy Layer up to 23-06-2026 EWS Income & Assets Certificate (valid for FY 2026-2027, based on FY 2025-2026 income, issued on or before 23-06-2026, on prescribed Haryana Government proforma) Disability Certificate (for PwBD candidates - in prescribed format as per RPwD Act, 2016) ESM Identity Card from Zila Sainik Board / Discharge Certificate or Book ESM Family Member Eligibility Certificate from Zila Sainik Board (valid as on 23-06-2026) Sports Certificate (for ESP candidates - as per applicable Haryana Government orders) Freedom Fighter Certificate from competent authority (for Freedom Fighter / children / grandchildren) No Objection Certificate (for in-service Government employees - from Head of Office/Department) Experience Certificate of equivalent post from Appointing Authority (for adhoc/contract/daily wages employees availing age relaxation) Any other documents i

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The High Court of Punjab and Haryana has announced a total of 1265 Clerk vacancies under the Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026. This includes 1015 existing posts and 250 anticipated vacancies up to June 2026. The number of vacancies may change depending on administrative requirements at the time of final selection

faq.question: Q2. What is the eligibility criteria for Haryana SSSC Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree (BA, BSc, or equivalent) from a recognized university along with basic computer proficiency in Word Processing and Spreadsheets. Additionally, applicants must have studied Hindi or Sanskrit at Matric level or higher. All qualifications must be completed before 09 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 42 years for General category candidates as on 01 January 2026. Reserved categories like SC, BC, PwBD, and others are eligible for age relaxation as per Haryana government rules. However, the upper age limit cannot exceed 52 years after all relaxations.

faq.question: Q4. What is the last date to apply for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process will start on 26 May 2026 at 4:00 PM and will remain open until 09 July 2026 at 4:00 PM. Candidates are advised to complete their application well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Late submissions will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of three stages: Computer Based Test (CBT), Computer Proficiency Test (CPT), and document verification. The final merit list is prepared based on marks obtained in the CBT only. The CPT is qualifying in nature and must be cleared to proceed further.

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam pattern of Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk CBT 2026?
faq.answer: The CBT includes General Knowledge and English Composition sections with both objective and subjective questions. The exam carries 100 marks with a duration of 2 hours, and there is negative marking of 1/4 for incorrect answers. Candidates must score at least 33% in each section and 40% overall to qualify.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for Haryana Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, the application fee varies based on category and gender. General category males need to pay Rs. 1200, while females pay Rs. 875. Haryana reserved categories pay Rs. 710, and PwBD candidates are exempted from paying any fee.

faq.question: Q8. Who can apply for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk posts under reserved categories?
faq.answer: Only bonafide residents of Haryana are eligible to claim reservation benefits under SC, BC, EWS, ESM, and other categories. Candidates from other states must apply under the General category even if they belong to a reserved category. Valid and updated certificates are mandatory at the time of application.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary of Haryana High Court Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: The pay scale for Clerk posts will be as per the latest rules and instructions issued by the Government of Haryana. Along with basic pay, selected candidates will also receive allowances and benefits applicable to state government employees. The job is permanent and offers stable career growth.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply online for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the official website sssc.gov.in by completing registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, and paying the fee. It is important to keep scanned documents, a valid email ID, and a working mobile number ready before applying. The application is considered complete only after successful fee payment.
